title: mulgedium roseum
intro: Mulgedium roseum, also known as mullein, is a flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. Mullein has been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and allergies, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its antioxidant and soothing effects. Mullein can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with herbal preparations often combining it with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
